title: Picaroon C. Boodle
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Kowakian monkey-lizard]] known as Pic|the Kowakian monkey-lizard [[Pikk Mukmuk]] or the [[death trooper]] [[Pik]]}}
{{Character
|type=Dark Jedi
|image=[[File:Pic.jpg]]
|name=Picaroon C. Boodle
|homeworld=
|birth=
|death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], ''[[Sulon Star]]'', [[Ruusan]]<ref name="DF2">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>
|species=Mutated [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]]<ref name="WOTC5">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}}</ref>
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]<ref name="WOTC5" />
|pronouns=He/him<ref name="WOTC5" />
|height=Less than 1 [[Meter/Legends|meter]]<ref name="WOTC5" />
|mass=
|hair=
|feathers=
|eyes=[[Color/Legends|Blue]]<ref name="WOTC5" />
|skin=Brown<ref name="JK2">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]''</ref>
|cyber=
|families=new
|parents=new
|partners=new
|siblings=new
|children=new
|affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="JK2" />
*[[Seven Dark Jedi]]<ref name="JK2" />
*[[Brothers of the Sith]]<ref name="JK2" />
|masters=*[[Cronal]]<ref name="WOTC5" />
*[[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]<ref name="WOTC5" />
*[[Sariss]]<ref name="WOTC5" />
|apprentices=
}}
{{Quote|It is a mystery why one would call Gorc and Pic twins. Even though one is a miniature version of the other, they look nothing alike. They also don't battle alike. Pic is the energy and Gorc, the counter. They are the clashing balance of opposites: one, the voice; the other, the body. This combination is deadly.|Qu Rahn as a Force ghost, to Kyle Katarn|audio=QuRahn-GorcPic.flac|<ref name="JK2" />}}
'''Picaroon C. Boodle''', later known as '''Pic''', was a mutated [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]] who, alongside his "[[Brothers of the Sith|brother]]" [[Gorc]], served as one of [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec's]] [[seven Dark Jedi]]. After being captured by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] for use as research subjects, Pic and Gorc were taken by Lord [[Cronal]], who trained and mutated them, before pressing them into the service of Jerec. The two [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] took on each other's genes and characteristics, and through a symbiotic link in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], they were able to sense each other's presence, emotions, and thoughts. Pic and his companion made a deadly pair: Pic's speed and intelligence when combined with Gorc's brute strength left many of their opponents dead during their time under Jerec.

During the search for the lost [[Valley of the Jedi]] in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Pic and Gorc were sent to destroy the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] and kill [[Kyle Katarn]] aboard the ship ''[[Sulon Star]]''. The two easily deactivated the droid, but Katarn managed to kill Gorc, who had underestimated the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agent. The death of his companion sent Pic into a frenzied rage, and the monkey-lizard viciously attacked Katarn, though he was eventually killed when the Rebel instinctively hit him with the heavy, dismembered head of 8t88.

==Biography==
===Early life===
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Kowakian monkey-lizard, Boodle left his homeworld early in his life and became the first mate (and pet) of the unusual and secretive <ref name="Secrets of the Sisar Run">''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]''</ref> [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Az-Iban]].<ref name="WOTC5" /> Together they piloted the [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400 light freighter]] ''[[Rampaging Ranat]]''<ref name="Secrets of the Sisar Run" /> through the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperspace runs]] until Picaroon was accused of carrying [[Cyborrean rabies]] and quarantined by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]].<ref name="WOTC5" />

Pic, as well as a giant [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] named Gorc who had been betrayed to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] by [[Thok (Gamorrean guard)/Legends|his brother]],<ref name="WOTC5" /> eventually became a research subject<ref name="NEGAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref> of [[Project Chubar]], an Imperial experiment run by [[Human/Legends|Human]] scientist [[Tuzin Gast]].<ref name="WOTC5" /> The project was a research effort intending to provide super-intelligent, loyal and Force-sensitive nonhumans to serve the Empire as [[Spy/Legends|spies]].<ref name="Wraith">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref>

===Sithspawn===
{{Quote|Beside me are the symbiotic twins Pic and Gorc. A rather interesting pair as you can see.|Lord Jerec to Qu Rahn|<ref name="Audio">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
[[File:Gorc_n_Pic.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Pic on Gorc's shoulders, alongside Boc Aseca.]]
[[Cronal]], also known as [[Blackhole]], was an expert in [[Intelligence/Legends|Intelligence]], [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] and genetic engineering who served with Imperial [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]. Blackhole was known as "monster maker" due to his skill to turn living things into [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] and needed [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] "animals" for his experiments. Pic and Gorc were chosen and transferred to Cronal, who experimented on the pair using powerful Sith alchemy, mutating Pic and Gorc beyond the point of recognition. They each shared in each other's genes, abilities and characteristics, as if they were genetic brothers and had a symbiotic link through the Force.<ref name="WOTC5" />

After being mutated and experimented on, Pic and his "twin" were handed over to Jerec, who, alongside his fellow Dark Jedi [[Sariss]], trained the two Sithspawn in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="WOTC5" /> Pic no longer resembled a brownish-skinned Kowakian monkey-lizard;<ref name="NEGAS" /> his skin had turned to a dark mixture between brown and green, and his head had become hairless,<ref name="WOTC5" /> unlike most other monkey-lizards. Pic and Gorc's bond meant they could fight together in unison,<ref name="WOTC5" /> and were described as being almost invincible when together.<ref name="DF2" /> Pic was also one of Lord Jerec's favored minions; the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] encouraged Pic's viciousness, and criticized him far less than most of the rest of his seven Dark Jedi. Jerec referred to Gorc and Pic almost with affection, as if they were his favorite pets, and described them as "quite an interesting pair."<ref name="Audio" />

===Search for the lost Valley of the Jedi===
{{Quote|You don't like Gorc? Then how about me!|Pic to Rolanda Gron|<ref name="Audio" />}}
The year after [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] died over [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], the two "twins" assisted Jerec in his search for the fabled Lost Valley of the Jedi. Jerec wanted to harness its power and re-establish the Empire, even though, after much searching, the location of the Valley eluded him. In 5 ABY, Pic and Gorc were stationed aboard Jerec's [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']] over the planet [[Dorlo]], where the Miraluka was searching for an old [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] named [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]], whom Jerec believed to have information about the Valley of the Jedi. On the surface of the planet, Rahn managed to evade three of Jerec's other underlings—the [[Epicanthix]] [[Yun]], Sariss and the giant [[Boltrunian/Legends|Boltrunian]] [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]]—and escaped on a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]. The corvette, however, was caught in the tractor beam of the ''Vengeance''. [[Boc Aseca]] and a group of soldiers then docked an assault [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] with the corvette, sent in [[sleeping gas]] and transferred the unconscious Rahn and his companions to the ''Vengeance''. The two Sithspawn found Rahn on the ship's deck. Pic then stole the old Jedi's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], which, although only Rahn knew at the time, had once belonged to the famous Jedi [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. Pic placed the silver-hilted [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber shoto]] on his belt, though he may never have actually used it before his death. Rahn and several others were then escorted to the bridge of the ''Vengeance''.<ref name="DF2" />

The duo were guarding the Jedi as he was recovering from the gas, and Pic thought he was taking too long to recover; he yelled at him to stand up in order to go and meet the others, threatening that Gorc would make him move, and he wouldn't like him. Rahn taunted the duo, saying that Gorc was either his pack animal, or the Pic himself was his trained [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|monkey-lizard]]; with that, Pic made Gorc to Rahn, so that he would appreciate their symbiotic relationship. Warning that they would detect if Rahn tried any [[Force power/Legends|Jedi trick]], they pushed him to a place where his friends were, and Pic announced the arrival of Jerec.<ref name="Audio" />

[[File:Pic-JK-Manualextract.png|thumb|right|Picaroon C. Boodle]]
On the bridge of his ship, Jerec began to question those who had been brought on board. One of them, [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] technologist [[Rolanda Gron]], defied Jerec, who then signaled to his group of Dark Jedi to execute the [[Alien/Legends|alien]]. The "twins" moved forward, as the Klatooinian unsuccessfully attempted to escape the hold the guards had on him. Just as Gorc was ready to strike the Rebel with his [[Lightclub|club-like lightsaber]], Pic lunged unexpectedly at Gron, stabbing him in the throat with his [[Sith sword|Sith dagger]],<ref name="DF2" /> earning Jerec's approval.<ref name="Audio" /> The tiny monkey-lizard then wiped his weapon off and walked away, leaving bloody trails as he scrambled back up onto Gorc's broad shoulders. Jerec had each of the remaining Rebels killed by his various underlings, until only Qu Rahn remained.<ref name="DF2" /> After much beating from Maw as well as from Jerec's other minions, still the old Jedi Master refused to reveal what he knew, and so Jerec used the Force to [[Drain Knowledge|rip it from Rahn's mind]]. With the information he needed in hand, the Miraluka was ready to dispose of Rahn, but the old Jedi Master, using the Force, grabbed hold of Yun's lightsaber, and began dueling with those around. Boc, Maw and Sariss were wounded or blocked, before Gorc rounded on Rahn. The old Jedi knocked Gorc's huge saber out of his hands, and just as Pic readied to strike, Jerec intervened. Although Rahn proved stronger than the group, he was no match for Jerec, who used the Force to send a [[Force Destruction|blast of energy]] that pushed the old Jedi down; and [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitate]] him within the next moments.<ref name="JK2" /><ref name="DF2" />

Jerec then obtained a coded map from the house of [[Morgan Katarn]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] and hired a droid named 8t88 to decipher it. 8t88 eventually decoded the map and sent it to Jerec, who had Pic and his Gamorrean companion,<ref name="DF2" /> backed up by an Imperial officer and two [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]],<ref name="WOTC4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale4|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 4}}</ref> meet the droid on the ''Sulon Star''. 8t88 expected to be rewarded for his success, but in actuality Pic had been sent to deactivate and destroy him.<ref name="DF2" /> The droid arrived on a shuttle and met Pic and Gorc in a dark corridor, asking for his pay. Pic sardonically told him they had it,<ref name="JK2" /> and then decapitated him.<ref name="WOTC4" />

===Final duel===
{{Quote|You killed Gorc! Now you will pay!|Pic after the death of his Gamorrean companion|<ref name="DF2" />}}
[[File:GorcAndPic-JKDFII.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Gorc and Pic face off against Kyle Katarn.]]
The one person who stood in Jerec's way to finding the Valley of the Jedi was Kyle Katarn, the son of Morgan Katarn. Kyle had tracked 8t88 to the ''Sulon Star'', looking for the information which would lead him to the Valley of the Jedi, where he knew he needed to go if he was to defeat Jerec.<ref name="DF2" /> Kyle, aided by the spirit of Qu Rahn,<ref name="WOTC4" /> arrived at the corridor 8t88 had been beheaded in, where Pic,<ref name="DF2" /> tasked with killing the Rebel agent,<ref name="WOTC4" /> was hiding on his fellow Sithspawn's shoulders in the shadows. As Kyle moved towards the droid, its head fell off its shoulders, landing on the floor, where 8t88's pet [[Hornagaunt]] lunged forward and digested it. Gorc attempted to crudely attack Katarn with his large weapon, overestimating the ease with which he would be able to squash his foe, and showed his lack of intelligence by standing in the way of Kyle's [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]]. The Rebel agent shot Gorc in the face, destroying the Gamorrean's brain and killing him.<ref name="DF2" />

Distraught at the loss of his companion, Pic lunged at Katarn, stabbing at him with his Sith dagger<ref name="WOTC5" /> and driving his talons into Kyle's back and neck as well as attempting to gouge his eyes out. Kyle managed to grab hold of and wrestle with one of the monkey-lizard's arms, and moved quickly backwards, driving Pic violently against the bulkhead and breaking at least one of the Dark Jedi's bones. Pic, who was in immense pain, sent a blast of Force energy at Katarn, and then fell unconscious for several minutes. When he awoke, Pic saw Katarn standing beside the dead body of the massive Hornagaunt, searching for 8t88's head. He launched himself at the Rebel, who instinctively used the head of 8t88 to bash away the tiny creature; Pic's skull was broken and he flew limply across the room, dead.<ref name="DF2" /><ref name="WOTC5" />

===Legacy===
Kyle Katarn went on to foil Jerec and defeat the other Dark Jedi.<ref name="JK2" /> Pic's body was left by Katarn on the ''Sulon Star'' along with Gorc's; Kyle did not realize at the time that the tiny [[darksider]] carried a weapon which had once belonged to Master Yoda. Later, after realizing the importance of the lightsaber, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] sent a group of Jedi to retrieve the lightsaber, though it was unknown if they succeeded; the weapon and Pic's body could have stayed intact on the ship, or someone else might have taken it.<ref name="WOTC5" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|What do I always tell you, Gorc? Teamwork, teamwork.|Picaroon C. Boodle|<ref name="Audio" />}}
Pic was a small creature less than a [[Meter/Legends|Imperial standard meter]] meter high, with a mass of twenty [[kilogram/Legends|kilograms]].<ref name="WOTC5" />

After being mutated, Pic was an unpleasant creature. He was extremely lively, and some described him as evil:<ref name="Manual">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' Instruction Manual''</ref> he liked killing, and often mocked those he had captured. The only person he seemed to actually care about was Gorc. Being in such close proximity to him, combined with the ability to see into his mind, Pic's bond to Gorc was more than just a dark side connection—it could almost have been described as a friendship. Gorc was dim-witted and slow, so Pic tried to look out for his companion's well-being. Even though he was the smaller of the two, Pic acted as a sort of "older brother" to his mutated friend.<ref name="WOTC5" /> He became angry when people abused Gorc,<ref name="DF2" /> and could usually be found hanging from Gorc's shoulders, often jumping from one to another.<ref name="JK2" /> Pic did all the talking; Gorc seemed barely capable of saying anything more than a grunt,<ref name="DF2" /> and preferred to remain quiet anyway.<ref name="Manual" /> In return for his kindness, Pic was given a miniature set of Gorc's red-brown [[M'uhk'gfa]] armor, which the Gamorrean had crafted himself, though without the dark helmet that Gorc chose to wear.<ref name="WOTC5" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Oh, good stab, Pic.|Gorc, to Pic, after the latter killed Rolanda Gron|<ref name="Audio" />}}
The Dark Jedi Jerec and Sariss taught each of their followers, who, including Pic and Gorc, were known as the seven Dark Jedi, much of the teachings of the [[Dark Force]]. Although they had an almost comical appearance (Pic was about a tenth of Gorc's weight and less than a third his height), the Brothers of the Sith were a deadly combination; Pic was agile, quick, and extremely intelligent, while Gorc was extremely strong, and neither of the two were afraid to slaughter their enemies. Gorc rarely moved or spoke, though when Pic stirred him up, the duo worked together as a team, covering all angles as well as each other's weaknesses.<ref name="Manual" /> Pic became skilled at using a Sith dagger,<ref name="WOTC5" /> although after he and Gorc captured Jedi Master Qu Rahn, Pic took one of the lightsabers he had carried;<ref name="DF2" /> an orange-bladed lightsaber shoto<ref name="JK2" /> which had once belonged to former Grand Master Yoda.<ref name="WOTC5" /> He adopted a style of combat to make up for his lack of height, jumping high into the air to stab at enemies' faces and throats, as well as making many wild swings with his lightsaber.<ref name="DF2" /><ref name="JK2" />

The [[Force-bond/Legends|bond]] between Pic and Gorc enabled them to work together with ease; at all times, both Pic and Gorc were aware of the other's location, sense, and feelings, and they even shared basic telepathy.<ref name="WOTC5" /> If the two were within several meters of each other, their powers were boosted,<ref name="WOTC4" /> though their closeness also meant that if one brother was injured, the pain was shared by the other. Every time Pic used the dark side, he further corrupted Gorc, and vice versa. As a Sith abomination, Pic slowly released the dark side of the Force, making both Force-users and non-Force-users feel afraid and intimidated.<ref name="WOTC5" />

Pic could use a variety of Force powers: he could heal himself or Gorc, enhance his abilities, and even spread a sense of fear onto his enemies using the dark side. He could also use the Force to grant him extra [[Force speed/Legends|speed]] and sense what was going on around him, as well as [[Mind trick/Legends#Force persuasion|affect]] the minds of enemies, which he used regularly to make himself appear invisible. He usually wielded his Sith dagger or stolen lightsaber, but he was more than capable of utilizing [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]], primitive weapons, and even [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]].<ref name="WOTC5" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Pic_jk.jpg|thumb|160px|Concept art for Pic.]]
Picaroon C. Boodle was created by [[Justin Chin]] for the [[1997]] PC game [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II|''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'']].<ref name="Manual" /> Realized by a CGI-character in the game and cutscenes, Pic was voiced by actor [[Denny Delk]].<ref name="JK2" /> He was drawn by artist [[Ezra Tucker]] in ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''.<ref name="DF2" />

Neither Pic's species nor his full name were ever stated in ''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'', his first appearance.<ref name="JK2" /> He was later referred to as a banshee in ''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'',<ref name="DF2" /> and later as a monkey-lizard in ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'', which also gave him his full name, Picaroon C. Boodle.<ref name="WOTC5" />

Pic's death in ''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'' is slightly different to his death in ''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' and in ''The Dark Forces Saga'', which is documented in this article. In the game, the player fights Pic and Gorc simultaneously, and either of them can be killed first. 8t88's pet Hornagaunt was also not present in the game.<ref name="JK2" />

According to [[Abel G. Peña]], Pic and Gorc may have been inspired by [[Wikipedia:Danny DeVito|Danny DeVito]] and [[Wikipedia:Arnold Schwarzenegger|Arnold Schwarzenegger]] in the [[1988]] comedy [[Wikipedia:Twins (1988 film)|''Twins'']], with one of the villains being tiny and the other huge.<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|6|Sithspawn!}}</ref>

When Abel Peña was writing the backstory for Pic and his companion for the ''Dark Forces Saga'', he re-used the term Sithspawn, originally a curse word used by [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] in ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'', which was revealed to mean a monstrosity created though dark side magic in ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''. Peña brought back the concept of Sithspawn, coupling two of the aliens seen in Jabba's Palace to create the "Brothers of the Sith."<ref name="Blog" />

The article [[Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|''Building a Better Jedi'']] that appeared in [[Star Wars Insider 31|''Star Wars Insider'' 31]] provided Pic's height as 4 [[Foot/Legends|feet]], which roughly equals 1.2 meters.<ref name="Google converter">{{WebCite|url=http://www.google.ru/intl/eng/help/features.html|text=Google unit converter|nobackup=1}}</ref> However, his entry in ''The Dark Forces Saga'' later contradicted that information, stating that Pic was less than a meter tall. This article uses the more recent ''The Dark Forces Saga''{{'s}} height.
